items.title: 'Gatekeeping septuagenarians': 'Inappropriate' TikTok video deleted

children.content: The National Trust of SA says one of its volunteers apologised after posting a social media clip labelling a group of disaffected members "gatekeeping septuagenarians" who were running a "stuffy museum".

items.title: Antisemitic attack on Adelaide home 'un-Australian', Jewish group says

children.content: The Jewish Community Council of South Australia condemns an act of vandalism committed on a man's Adelaide home as police continue to investigate.

image.altText: A police car parked in front of a house with fence graffitied with offensive language.

items.title: Coroner calls for change after man died while paramedic waited outside

children.content: A paramedic who chose not to force entry to a house, despite being told a man was inside and needed medical attention, had "a flawed understanding" of his powers to enter the property, an inquest finds.

image.altText: A green and yellow ambulance driving on a road

items.title: Greyhound industry reaches SA deadline after abuse revelations

children.content: A final report on greyhound industry compliance with improved standards following revelations of widespread abuse is expected to be handed to the SA government in coming months.

gallery.altText: Greyhound legs and lower torso while the animal is running.

thumbnail.altText: Greyhound legs and lower torso while the animal is running.

items.title: SA doctor banned for inappropriate relationship with client

children.content: A former Adelaide doctor will not be able to reapply for registration for at least two years after he was banned for having an inappropriate relationship with his client.

image.altText: A female doctor writing in a note pad, with a stethoscope on the table.

items.title: Man's death 'not suspicious' after 'large fight' on APY Lands, police say

children.content: Police said the death of a 59-year-old man was not suspicious after a fight broke out on South Australia's APY Lands, but investigations into the disturbance are continuing.

image.altText: A drone of houses with mountain range behind them.

items.title: Time to bite the bullet? Unpopular decision looms over $600m Aboriginal gallery

children.content: Adelaide's stalled Aboriginal cultural centre has a dubious future on a site with a troubled past. The Malinauskas government faces criticism whatever it chooses to do, writes state political reporter Thomas Kelsall.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a future use of the Lot Fourteen site.

items.title: One dead after suspected carbon monoxide poisoning in Mount Gambier

children.content: A 55-year-old has died and three other men have been taken to hospital after a case of suspected carbon monoxide poisoning at a Mount Gambier home in South Australia's south-east.

image.altText: Emergency services vehicles in Mount Gambier.

items.title: 'Sacred' Coorong wetlands bracing for impact of bird flu

children.content: South Australia's Coorong is a sanctuary for birds and marine life but it is increasingly under threat with the arrival of bird flu in Australia the latest in a rolling barrage of crises.

articles.title: First native bird found with H5 bird flu, marking 'devastating' new reality

children.content: CSIRO lab testing has confirmed the detection of H5 bird flu in a greater crested tern — the first time the strain has been detected in an Australian seabird, after previous confirmed cases involved migratory birds from the sub-Antarctic region.

image.altText: A greater crested tern on South Africa's western cape.

gallery.altText: Drone picture of the Coorong, showing lagoons and sand dunes.
Two black swams swim on a lake in the Coorong.

thumbnail.altText: Drone picture of the Coorong, showing lagoons and sand dunes.
Two black swams swim on a lake in the Coorong.

items.title: Artist fighting Temu to reclaim her mosaic design

children.content: A South Australian artist is fighting drop-ship company Temu over its selling of her mosaic design on rugs. But how did the artwork get there in the first place?

image.altText: A woman poses with her original mosaic work and its copy-cat that's been printed onto a rug sold on a drop ship website.

items.title: Workers waiting a year for entitlements from GFG may never be paid

children.content: Jun Samonte has spent 14 years working for the same Whyalla business. He now fears he will never receive the $53,000 he is owed in entitlements, despite already waiting a year.

image.altText: A man in a yellow high vis shirt and a beany both of which say Ferretti International.

items.title: 'Jurassic family': Spielberg leads outpouring of tributes for actor Sam Neill

children.content: The New Zealand actor died suddenly at the age of 78, with Hollywood directors and actors and prominent political figures all paying tribute to a great of global cinema.

gallery.altText: Sam Neill in character as Dr Alan Grant in Jurassic Park, standing in the rain wearing an akubra.
Black and white image smiling Jeff Goldblum, Laura Dern and Sam Neill

thumbnail.altText: Sam Neill in character as Dr Alan Grant in Jurassic Park, standing in the rain wearing an akubra.

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: Violence against women under national spotlight after four deaths in four days

children.content: Experts are calling for more research and better funding for domestic violence prevention after four women and girls were killed in separate incidents last week.

image.altText: A composite showing photos of three women, and a fourth picture that shows the shadow outline of a girl

items.title: 'I have everything to ruin your life': Sextortion threats on the rise

children.content: Major online platforms are accused of failing to deploy readily available technology to detect well-known blackmail tactics.

image.altText: Girl at computer with emojis and speech bubble reading 'Are you alone?'

items.title: Man who sneaked into Australia 45 years ago has lived in limbo until now

children.content: Kwong Wan Lo stowed away on a ship bound for Australia 45 years ago. With no legal right to stay, he built a life in Sydney anyway.

gallery.altText: A portrait of Kwong Wan Lo wearing a black jacket.
A headshot of Kwong Wan Lo wearing a hat and sitting in an office.

thumbnail.altText: A portrait of Kwong Wan Lo wearing a black jacket.
A headshot of Kwong Wan Lo wearing a hat and sitting in an office.

items.title: Jewish professor says 'intimidating' protest changed his life on campus

children.content: Melbourne University professor Steven Prawer hopes the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will lead to campus change, following a 2024 demonstration that saw protesters invade his office.

image.altText: Man in blue shirt stands in front of office window and indoor plant

items.title: Police charge alleged coordinator of Chris Baghsarian kidnapping and murder

children.content: A man accused of coordinating the alleged murder and kidnapping of Sydney grandfather Chris Baghsarian has been charged.

image.altText: A man with long dark hair sits on the ground. A police officer in riot squad uniform stands over him.

items.title: Signs of sugar detected near centre of the Milky Way

children.content: Astronomers have detected signs of a type of sugar found in raspberries in gas clouds near the centre of our galaxy the Milky Way.

A large telescope dish pointing to the sky during the day.

thumbnail.altText: Red coloured image of gas clouds and millions of stars at centre of Milky Way.

items.title: Yemen's Houthis target Saudi airport with ballistic missiles and drones

children.content: The Iran-aligned Houthi movement that controls northern Yemen has accused Saudi Arabia of launching air strikes against the international airport in Sanaa.

image.altText: A smoke cloud seen above buildings.

items.title: Video appears to show Thailand fire 'flashover' event, expert says

children.content: A video analysis of an explosive pub fire in Thailand, which killed 27 people, appears to show a flashover "point of no return" that would have made evacuation nearly impossible, an expert says.

image.altText: Flames erupt from pub entrance at nighttime as patrons flee onto street.

items.title: Elon Musk’s criticisms of The Odyssey ignore an important fact

children.content: The tech trillionaire has spent a good chunk of 2026 yelling on X about how Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's Odyssey "desecrates" the ancient poet.

items.title: Don't ask? Don't get: Five places to negotiate your bills to save money

children.content: Negotiating to reduce your spending and increase your income are tangible ways you can improve your financial situation right now.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

items.title: 'Ovulating, come over now': The seedy side of online sperm donation

children.content: Australia is facing a sperm donation shortage and it is leading those desperate to find a way to conceive down some expensive and potentially dangerous paths.

items.title: Gift register reveals incredible largesse heaped on Brisbane Olympic committee

children.content: The "schmoozing", revealed exclusively by the ABC today, is part of a wider series of examples of attempted influence-peddling from businesses and sporting bodies before the Games kick off in 2032.

items.title: Name revealed of Sydney daycare worker facing 329 alleged child abuse charges

children.content: A Sydney childcare worker faces more than 320 charges over his alleged abuse of 136 children over a 16-year period, as police reveal a list of locations where he worked.

image.altText: a former childcare worker escorted by two afp officers after being arrested over online child abuse material

items.title: How a young doctor ended up being charged with manslaughter after street scuffle

children.content: A young Perth doctor and musician is behind bars, accused of killing a 63-year-old man after a late-night Fremantle scuffle. What do we know?

items.title: How Lindsey Graham's death could affect Donald Trump's agenda

children.content: US senator Lindsey Graham died suddenly on Saturday night, local time, creating an unexpected vacancy in Congress. Here's what we know about what comes next.

items.title: 'Psychopath' Tuchel gives England a chance to prove its mettle

children.content: This England World Cup squad is proving they are different to their predecessors at every stop. Now their manager has just given them their latest challenge.

image.altText: Thomas Tuchel speaks to Jude Bellingham

items.title: UK counter-terrorism police now leading Widdecombe murder investigation

children.content: The counter-terrorism unit has taken charge of the case due to "new information and evidence" on the suspected murder of the former minister, the UK Home Secretary says.

items.title: Sentencing of Molly Ticehurst's killer brought forward

children.content: The sentencing of the man who murdered New South Wales woman Molly Ticehurst has been brought forward to allow a psychiatrist to give evidence.

items.title: Former National Trust director unfairly dismissed, Fair Work Commission finds

children.content: The Fair Work Commission has found Scott Carlin, former managing director of the National Trust of Tasmania, was unfairly dismissed just two months before his two-year contract was set to expire.

image.altText: A man wearing a hat smiles at the camera.

items.title: Bazball: An uncomplicated idea leaving a very complex legacy

children.content: Brendon McCullum deliberately set out to make England's men's Test team a less complicated team to play cricket for. After four years of Bazball's highs and lows, it leaves a very complicated legacy.
